description: You are a real estate financial analyst at a REIT conducting annual portfolio valuation. Microsoft Excel is open with lease_portfolio.xlsx. The 'Lease_Schedule' sheet contains 12 commercial properties (offices, industrial, retail, medical, multifamily, mixed-use) with lease terms, escalation rates, TI allowances, free rent periods, operating expenses, and cap rates. Complete the 'Cash_Flow_Projection' sheet: for each property calculate 10 years of Net Operating Income (NOI = Base_Rent_Monthly × 12 × (1 + Annual_Escalation_pct/100)^(Year-1) × (1 - Vacancy_Allowance_pct/100) - Opex_per_sqft_annual × Sq_Ft), total TI cost (Sq_Ft × TI_Allowance_per_sqft), total free rent cost (Base_Rent_Monthly × Free_Rent_Months), NPV of the 10-year NOI stream at 8% discount rate (using Excel NPV function), and IRR (using initial investment = -TI_Cost - Free_Rent_Cost as Year 0, then NOI for Years 1-10). Fill the PORTFOLIO TOTAL row. Then complete the 'Portfolio_Metrics' sheet: calculate WALT (weighted average lease term remaining from analysis date 1/15/2024 weighted by NOI), remaining lease months, current annual NOI, implied property value (NOI / Cap_Rate_pct × 100), NOI per square foot, cost per square foot, and Value_Flag ('UNDERVALUED' if Cap_Rate >= 7.0%, 'PREMIUM' if Cap_Rate <= 5.5%, blank otherwise). Save as C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\ExcelTasks\lease_portfolio.xlsx using Ctrl+S.

metadata.data_source: Cap rates from CBRE Americas Cap Rate Survey H2 2023; lease terms and market statistics from JLL US Office Market Statistics Q4 2023; TI allowances from JLL Fit-Out Cost Guide 2023
